name: Pub/Sub Edge (MQTT/NATS-style)
strengths: No polling — events arrive when they happen. Tiny per-event overhead. One TCP connection carries thousands of topic subscriptions. Already the dominant pattern for IoT and finance.
tradeoffs: Broker is a single point of failure (federate to fix). Subscribers are stateful — broker tracks who wants what. No native partition tolerance.
real_world_examples: MQTT for IoT (AWS IoT Core, Mosquitto). NATS at fintech. Server-sent events on news sites. WebSockets in chat apps.
